content: Thanks for replying. One more thing. Even if you don't answer it's fine cause this question doesn't even have any thing to do with your background. As I mentioned earlier that another person will handle the answers through screen share. Now, the TOEFL test have 4 sections - Listening, Reading, Writing and speaking. The listening, reading and writing section can be managed because it comprises of MCQ and select one word answers. When it comes to speaking section, The toefl has 4 types of questions - Type 1 - Speak on the topic for 45 seconds. Type 2 - There will be a short article or material followed by an audio being played related to same material. Then the we nee to answer for 60 seconds.  Type 3 - Same as type 2 but more generalised topic.  Type 4 - Sumarise the academic lecture being played and speak for 60 seconds.                                                                        During these speaking part, how will the person sitting outside the room will give answers to the candidate. My first thought was to copy paste  the answers on the transparent browser but that can be visible in the second camera. I want to know upto which level the browser can become transparent. @bizarro12345 if you share a pic of the minimum level of transparency, it'll help a lot. If you got any other ideas to help.
Yes, it can go fullscreen so that it looks just like a normal pc screen

Exams like Duolingo english test and Toelf IBT home edition require to put secondary camera in front of the laptop so that the proctor can see if the candidate is really typing and not using any other material. One way to cheat during these exams is to screen share the host system to another pc and install the EXE file in the Virtual machine. My concern is whether the Vm layout can go beyond the screen size (slightly bigger) so that the box in not seen and it appears as if the VM is the original one to the secondary camera? This way another person outside the room can access the questions and then provide answers.
